To elucidate the semiconducting properties of NML and co-incorporated ones upon light excitation, the Mott-Schottky method was applied to estimate the flat band positions of the photocatalysts [1]. 换一种表述

In order to clarify the semiconducting characteristics of NML and those with co-incorporation when excited by light, the flat band potential of the photocatalysts was determined using the Mott-Schottky approach [1].

Steady-state photoluminescence spectroscopy (PL) is a productive strategy to show the transfer and separation efficiency of photogenerated charge carriers, in which weaker PL intensity suggests a greater separation rate of photoexcited charge carriers [1, 2].  换一种表述

Steady-state photoluminescence (PL) spectroscopy serves as an effective technique for illustrating the efficiency of transfer and separation of photogenerated charge carriers, where a lower PL intensity implies an increased rate of separation of the photoinduced charges [1, 2].

Steady-state photoluminescence spectroscopy (PL) is a productive strategy to show the transfer and separation efficiency of photogenerated charge carriers, in which weaker PL intensity suggests a greater separation rate of photoexcited charge carriers [1, 2]. 换一种表述

Steady-state photoluminescence spectroscopy (PL) acts as a valuable method for demonstrating the efficacy with which photogenerated charge carriers are transferred and separated, evidenced by the fact that a diminished PL intensity indicates a higher rate of charge carrier separation following photoexcitation [1, 2].
